edges.description: Defective B-cell differentiation and class switching impair antibody production, producing hypogammaglobulinemia.
edges.source: Defective B Cell Differentiation and Class-Switch Recombination

edges.description: Impaired class-switch recombination reduces IgG production.
edges.source: Defective B Cell Differentiation and Class-Switch Recombination

edges.description: Impaired class-switch recombination reduces IgA production.
edges.source: Defective B Cell Differentiation and Class-Switch Recombination

edges.description: Antibody-production failure predisposes CVID patients to recurrent infections.
intermediate_mechanisms: Impaired antibody production reduces humoral protection against pathogens.
edges.source: Defective B Cell Differentiation and Class-Switch Recombination

edges.description: Immune dysregulation in CVID can manifest as autoimmune cytopenias, including autoimmune hemolytic anemia.
Immune dysregulation in CVID can manifest as autoimmune thrombocytopenia.
CVID immune dysregulation can present with hepatosplenomegaly.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lymphoproliferative immune dysregulation can enlarge lymphoid organs.

edges.description: CVID-associated granulomatous inflammation produces the granulomatous disease phenotype.
The same granulomatous and lymphocytic immune pathology can manifest as GLILD.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: Patient B.I.1, previously diagnosed with common variable immunodeficiency (CVID), had hepatosplenomegaly, autoimmune hemolytic anemia (AIHA), autoimmune thrombocytopenia, pulmonary nodules, and cerebral infiltrative lesions.

nodes.description: The hallmark of CVID is a failure of B cells to differentiate into immunoglobulin-secreting plasma cells. Defective class-switch recombination leads to impaired production of IgG, IgA, and often IgM. Most patients show reduced switched memory B cells (CD27+IgD-IgM-). Defects in B cell receptor signaling, BAFF/APRIL axis, and NF-kB signaling all converge on impaired germinal center output.
nodes.id: Defective B Cell Differentiation and Class-Switch Recombination

evidence.reference_title: The Scope and Impact of Viral Infections in Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) and CVID-like Disorders: A Literature Review
evidence.snippet: The pathogenesis of CVID involves complex immunological dysregulation, including defects in B cell development, antibody class switching, and plasma cell differentiation. These abnormalities contribute to an impaired humoral immune response against viral agents, predisposing individuals with CVID to a broad range of viral infections.

evidence.reference_title: TACI is mutant in common variable immunodeficiency and IgA deficiency.
evidence.snippet: B cells from individuals with TACI mutations expressed TACI but did not produce IgG and IgA in response to the TACI ligand APRIL, probably reflecting impaired isotype switching.

evidence.reference_title: The biological basis for current treatment strategies for granulomatous disease in common variable immunodeficiency
evidence.snippet: This review describes the current knowledge on the pathogenesis of granuloma formation in CVID and the biological basis of the current treatment options.

nodes.description: CVID-associated granulomatous and lymphocytic interstitial lung disease (GLILD) involves poorly organized granulomas with abundant T and B cells. Key features include Th1 polarization with elevated IFN-gamma and sIL-2R, B cell abnormalities with elevated BAFF and CD21low B cells, and innate immune alterations including M2 macrophage skewing and neutrophil dysregulation.

evidence.reference_title: The biological basis for current treatment strategies for granulomatous disease in common variable immunodeficiency
evidence.snippet: Histological analysis shows that T and B cells are abundantly present in the granulomas that are less well organized and are frequently associated with lymphoid hyperplasia. Increased presence of activation markers such as soluble IL-2 receptor (sIL-2R) and IFN-ɣ, suggest increased Th1-cell activity. Moreover, B-cell abnormalities are prominent in CVID, with elevated IgM, BAFF, and CD21low B cells correlating with granulomatous disease progression.

nodes.description: CVID-associated GLILD is a noninfectious interstitial lung complication characterized by granulomatous and lymphocytic inflammation.

evidence.reference_title: The biological basis for current treatment strategies for granulomatous disease in common variable immunodeficiency
evidence.snippet: Our review links the biological context of CVID with granulomatous disease or GLILD to currently prescribed therapies and potential targeted treatments.

evidence.reference_title: The Scope and Impact of Viral Infections in Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) and CVID-like Disorders: A Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) is a heterogeneous primary immunodeficiency disorder characterised by impaired antibody production, leading to recurrent infections and an increased susceptibility to viral pathogens.

nodes.description: Approximately 25-50% of CVID patients develop autoimmune complications, most commonly autoimmune cytopenias (immune thrombocytopenia and autoimmune hemolytic anemia). This reflects a breakdown of peripheral tolerance and defective regulatory T cell function, particularly through the CTLA-4/LRBA checkpoint axis. Expansion of autoreactive CD21low B cells contributes to antibody-mediated autoimmunity.

evidence.reference_title: Immune dysregulation in human subjects with heterozygous germline mutations in CTLA4.
evidence.snippet: human CTLA4 haploinsufficiency caused dysregulation of FoxP3(+) regulatory T (Treg) cells, hyperactivation of effector T cells, and lymphocytic infiltration of target organs. Patients also exhibited progressive loss of circulating B cells, associated with an increase of predominantly autoreactive CD21(lo) B cells and accumulation of B cells in nonlymphoid organs.

evidence.reference_title: The Scope and Impact of Viral Infections in Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) and CVID-like Disorders: A Literature Review
evidence.snippet: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) is a heterogeneous primary immunodeficiency disorder characterised by impaired antibody production, leading to recurrent infections and an increased susceptibility to viral pathogens.
